title: President Trump Talks to College Champions at the White House
summary: President Donald Trump spoke to college athletes at the White House. He honored about 100 athletes from seven teams that won national championships in 2025. The teams included Oklahoma State in men's golf, Texas A&M in women's volleyball, Wake Forest in men's tennis, Georgia in women's tennis, Youngstown State in women's bowling, Florida State in women's soccer, and West Virginia in mixed rifle. Trump talked about new rules for college sports. He wants Congress to make laws to help control how athletes move between schools and get paid. He signed an order to limit how long athletes can play and protect women's sports. Some people think these rules might not be legal, so they need laws to make them stronger. Trump said it's not fair for young athletes to compete against older ones.

background_read: College sports are games played by students at universities. The NCAA is a group that makes rules for these sports. Athletes can win championships, which are big awards. Sometimes, athletes want to change schools or get paid for playing, and there are rules about this. The White House is where the President lives and works in Washington, D.C. Congress is part of the government that creates laws. An executive order is a rule made by the President without Congress, but it might need laws to make it stronger.
Article_Structure: WHO: President Donald Trump and about 100 college athletes from championship teams
WHAT: Trump spoke at the White House, honoring athletes and discussing new rules for college sports
WHERE: The White House in Washington, D.C.
WHY: To address issues like athlete transfers and pay, and to push for laws to support these rules

perspectives.description: Some people think new rules are needed to make college sports fair and protect athletes.
Others worry that the rules might not be legal and need laws to be enforced properly.
